pi_items.source: GitHub raw content from https://github.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge - FlightPlan model is defined in gcs_operations/models.py, not in registry/models.py. Registry app does not contain a FlightPlan model.

pi_items.verified_value: FlightPlan exists but in gcs_operations app, and fields do not match task assumptions
pi_items.source: GitHub source code (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge/master/gcs_operations/models.py). Real FlightPlan has fields: id, name, plan_file_json, geo_json, is_editable, created_at, updated_at. Task assumes: name, start_time, end_time, status, aircraft, pilot - none of these extra fields exist in the real model.

pi_items.verified_value: Path structure is correct for a Django management command, but the registry app is the wrong app for FlightPlan model
pi_items.source: Django management command structure. Real FlightPlan is in gcs_operations app, so command should be at /opt/aerobridge/gcs_operations/management/commands/close_expired_plans.py

privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetic/fabricated Aerobridge environment. The real Aerobridge project (openskies-sh/aerobridge, archived Dec 2023) defines FlightPlan in the gcs_operations app with fields: name, plan_file_json, geo_json, is_editable, created_at, updated_at. The task assumes FlightPlan is in the registry app with fields: name, start_time, end_time, status, aircraft, pilot. The task's setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts attempt to create and query FlightPlan records with these non-existent fields, indicating the task is testing a modified/synthetic version of Aerobridge that does not match the actual open-source project.
